audits.summary: No publicly linked or published audit report was found in the rendernetwork GitHub repositories or project documentation for the original RNDR token contract deployed in 2020.
The foundation-dao-smart-contracts repo contains no audit subfolder or linked report as of the research date; no third-party audit is publicly disclosed.

exploit_history.description: No exploits, hacks, or security incidents involving Render Network smart contracts appear on Rekt.news or DefiLlama's hacks tracker as of research date. RNDR is primarily a utility/payment token with limited on-chain DeFi surface area, reducing exploit exposure.
exploit_history.post_mortem_url: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no incidents found on https://rekt.news or https://defillama.com/hacks

code_health.contributors_count: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — GitHub API rate-limited without auth; visible contributors on foundation-dao repo appear to be 3–5 based on public contributor tab
code_health.weekly_commits_avg: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — requires GitHub API or authenticated access for accurate stats; repos appear low-activity (token contract is static)
code_health.last_commit_date: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — public repo shows infrequent commits; foundation-dao repo last visible activity was 2023 based on public browsing
code_health.bug_bounty_platform: NOT_AVAILABLE_FREE_TIER — no active listing found on Immunefi (https://immunefi.com/explore/) or HackerOne public pages as of research date

dependencies.notes: Wormhole suffered a $320M exploit in Feb 2022 (unrelated to RNDR). RNDR's migration to Solana and cross-chain liquidity relies on Wormhole infrastructure, inheriting its bridge risk. Source: https://rekt.news/wormhole-rekt/
Original RNDR token is a standard ERC-20 on Ethereum mainnet; inherits Ethereum consensus security. Contract: 0x6De037ef9aD2725EB40118Bb1702EBb27e4Aeb24

Render Network migrated to Solana in late 2023 (RENDER token). Solana has experienced multiple network outages (2022–2023), introducing liveness risk for the payment layer. Source: https://docs.rendernetwork.com

RNDR/RENDER token contracts do not appear to use on-chain price oracles directly; pricing for GPU job payments is handled off-chain by the Render platform, reducing oracle attack surface. No Chainlink or Pyth dependency confirmed in public contracts.

rationale: Render Network's on-chain security surface is relatively narrow — the core asset is a standard ERC-20/SPL token with no complex DeFi logic, no known exploits, and no entries on Rekt.news or DefiLlama hacks. However, the security posture is materially weakened by the absence of any publicly disclosed smart contract audits, no active bug bounty program on Immunefi or equivalent platforms, and inherited bridge risk from Wormhole for cross-chain RNDR/RENDER transfers. The migration to Solana adds network-level liveness risk but does not introduce new smart contract complexity.
